title: Georgia Senators Ask Questions About a Trump Case
summary: In Georgia, state senators asked Nathan Wade, a former prosecutor, about his work on a case involving former President Donald Trump and the 2020 election. The senators wanted to know about talks his team had with federal investigators, but Wade said he couldn't remember many details. This happened at a hearing where a committee was looking into how the case was handled. The case was about Trump and others trying to change election results in Georgia. Earlier, the prosecutor leading the case, Fani Willis, was removed because she had a relationship with Wade, which some thought was a problem. Wade defended the team's work, saying they did it on their own without outside help. The senators focused on billing records and meetings, but Wade kept saying he didn't recall specifics.

What did the senators focus on during the hearing?

background_read: In Georgia, after the 2020 presidential election, there were claims that some people, including former President Donald Trump, tried to illegally change the results. A prosecutor named Fani Willis started a case against them using a law against organized crime. She hired Nathan Wade to help, but they had a personal relationship, which caused problems. A committee of state senators was formed to look into how this was handled, and they held hearings to ask questions about it. This article talks about one of those hearings where Wade answered questions.
Article_Structure: WHO: Georgia state senators and former special prosecutor Nathan Wade
WHAT: A hearing where senators asked Wade about his work on the Trump election case and his billing records, but he said he couldn't remember details
WHERE: Atlanta, Georgia, at a state Senate committee meeting
WHY: To investigate how the case was handled and if there were any problems like conflicts of interest

perspectives.description: They want to find out if there was wrongdoing in how the case was run, especially with Wade's hiring and communications.
He defends the team's work as independent and says he doesn't recall specifics about meetings or billing.
